items.q: Does globalisation benefit all places equally?
items.correct: No — flows of trade and investment concentrate in some regions while others are switched off; globalisation creates winners and losers between and within countries.
items.misconception: Growth in global trade automatically lifts every country in the same way.
items.random_wrong: Globalisation only affects countries on the equator.
items.why: Uneven outcomes are central to evaluating global systems; students often assume a tide that lifts all boats.

items.q: Is global governance the same as world government?
items.correct: No — global governance is the web of institutions, laws and norms, such as the UN and WTO, through which states cooperate; no single authority rules over states.
items.misconception: The United Nations can force any country to obey its decisions.
items.random_wrong: Global governance is run by the world's largest TNC.
items.why: Students overestimate the enforcement power of international institutions, missing why governance often fails.

items.q: Do TNCs keep every stage of production in their home country?
items.correct: No — TNCs typically spread production through global supply chains, locating each stage where costs, skills and market access are best, while headquarters and research often stay at home.
items.misconception: A company's products are all made in the country where the brand is based.
items.random_wrong: TNCs are banned from operating in more than three countries.
items.why: The spatial division of labour explains how global production networks actually work.
Correct answer: McLean. “Bezos” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What does the KOF Index measure?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Globalisation. “Poverty” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Top 500 TNCs control roughly what percentage of trade?
items.why: Exact answer: 70 %. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
